fields.summary: Implement typed LRU cache module with TTL eviction and full test suite
fields.description:
## Overview

Implement a production-quality LRU (Least-Recently-Used) cache module in TypeScript with TTL (time-to-live) support and a comprehensive Vitest test suite.

## Files to create

- `src/cache/types.ts` — TypeScript interfaces and types
- `src/cache/lru-cache.ts` — core LRU cache implementation
- `src/cache/index.ts` — public re-exports
- `src/cache/lru-cache.test.ts` — comprehensive Vitest test suite (target: 30+ tests)
- Update `src/index.ts` to re-export from `src/cache/index.ts`

## Detailed Requirements

### Types (`src/cache/types.ts`)

```typescript
export interface CacheOptions {
  capacity: number;           // max number of entries; must be >= 1
  defaultTtlMs?: number;      // optional default TTL in milliseconds
}

export interface CacheEntry<V> {
  value: V;
  expiresAt: number | null;   // Date.now() + ttl, or null if no TTL
}

export interface CacheStats {
  size: number;       // current number of entries
  capacity: number;   // max capacity
  hits: number;       // total cache hits
  misses: number;     // total cache misses
  evictions: number;  // total LRU evictions
  expirations: number;// total TTL expirations
}
```

### LRU Cache class (`src/cache/lru-cache.ts`)

Implement `class LRUCache<K, V>` with the following public API:

```typescript
constructor(options: CacheOptions)

get(key: K): V | undefined
set(key: K, value: V, ttlMs?: number): void
has(key: K): boolean
delete(key: K): boolean
clear(): void

get size(): number
get capacity(): number
stats(): CacheStats
keys(): K[]
values(): V[]
entries(): [K, V][]
```

### LRU Eviction Policy

- When `set()` would exceed capacity, evict the least-recently-used entry first
- Both `get()` and `set()` on an existing key count as a use (move to most-recent position)
- Use a doubly-linked list + Map internally for O(1) get and set

### TTL Behaviour

- If `ttlMs` is passed to `set()`, entry expires after that many milliseconds
- If not passed, use `options.defaultTtlMs` if set; otherwise no expiry
- `get()` on an expired entry must: delete the entry, increment expirations, return undefined
- `has()` must return false for expired entries

### Stats Tracking

- hits: incremented every time get() returns a value
- misses: incremented every time get() returns undefined (missing OR expired)
- evictions: incremented every time an entry is evicted due to capacity
- expirations: incremented every time an expired entry is discovered

## Test Requirements (`src/cache/lru-cache.test.ts`)

Write a Vitest test suite covering at minimum:

1. Basic operations - set/get/has/delete/clear
2. LRU eviction order - correct entry is evicted
3. Access updates recency - get() moves entry to most-recent
4. TTL expiry - expired entries return undefined
5. Default TTL - defaultTtlMs applies when no per-entry ttl
6. Stats accuracy - hits, misses, evictions, expirations
7. Capacity=1 edge case
8. Update existing key - set() on existing key updates value, does NOT evict
9. Delete - removed entries don't count toward size
10. keys/values/entries return items in LRU order
11. Generic types - works with different key/value types
12. size getter reflects live entries only

## Acceptance Criteria

- pnpm build passes with no TypeScript errors
- pnpm test passes
- pnpm lint passes
- No any types in src/cache/*.ts
- All public API surface has JSDoc comments
- LRUCache exported from src/index.ts
